current_run.description: Given an N-qubit oracle f(x), find the N-bit secret s. The oracle computes f(x) = popcount( (x & (x ROR 1)) ^ (x & s) ) % 2, where x ROR 1 is a cyclic right shift of x by 1 bit, and popcount is the Hamming weight. The function combines a fixed quadratic term (cyclic adjacent bit product parity) with a secret-dependent linear term (Bernstein-Vazirani-like). The goal is to determine the secret s with minimal queries.
